output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Get It Back in 60 Seconds
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Know your tenant rights and send a legal demand letter in 60 seconds for just $19.99—no signup needed.
content_outline: Signs Your Landlord Is Wrongfully Withholding Your Deposit
What State Laws Say About Security Deposit Deadlines and Deductions
How a Demand Letter Gets Results Faster Than Begging
Step-by-Step: Send Your Letter in 60 Seconds With Deposits-Back.com
key_points: Most U.S. states require landlords to return security deposits within 14–30 days after move-out.
Landlords cannot legally deduct for normal wear and tear or charge vague 'cleaning' fees without proof.
A formal letter of demand is often the fastest, cheapest way to recover a withheld deposit before going to court.
Deposits-Back.com lets tenants generate and send a state-specific demand letter in 60 seconds for a one-time $19.99 fee.
output.cta: Don't wait months for money that belongs to you. Create your security deposit demand letter at Deposits-Back.com in 60 seconds for just $19.99—no sign-up, no subscription, just results.
output.target_audience: Frustrated U.S. renters whose landlords are wrongfully withholding, delaying, or refusing to return their security deposit.

social_hooks.hook_text: POV: You moved out 3 weeks ago and your landlord just ghosted your deposit.
If your landlord hasn't returned your deposit in 30 days, they're already breaking the law.
Your landlord can't keep your security deposit for 'normal wear and tear.' That's literally illegal.
Stop asking your landlord for your deposit back. Start demanding it.
My landlord tried to charge me $1,200 for a 'cleaning fee.' Here's what I sent him instead.

short_form_scripts.title: The 30-Day Rule
short_form_scripts.script: If your landlord hasn't returned your deposit in 30 days, they're already breaking the law. Most states give them 14 to 30 days max. After that? Penalties. You don't need a lawyer. You need a demand letter. Deposits-Back.com. $19.99. 60 seconds. No signup. Get your money.

short_form_scripts.title: Ghosted Deposit
short_form_scripts.script: POV: You moved out three weeks ago and your landlord just ghosted your deposit. Texting 'where's my money?' isn't working. A formal demand letter hits different—it shows you know your rights. Create yours at Deposits-Back.com in 60 seconds. One-time $19.99.

short_form_scripts.title: Normal Wear and Tear
short_form_scripts.script: Your landlord cannot keep your deposit for normal wear and tear. That's illegal. But they'll try if you don't call them out. Send a demand letter that cites your state laws. Go to Deposits-Back.com. $19.99. Done in a minute.
